title: Android-Based Ethnomathematics Learning Media Development: A Systematic Literature Review
author: Torang Siregar
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This study aims to map the direction and trends of research related to the development of Android-based learning media containing ethnomathematics in mathematics education. The method used is a  Systematic Literature Review (SLR)  with the  PRISMA  protocol. The analysis was conducted on  14 articles  that had been selected according to predetermined characteristics. The results indicate the use of four primary development models:  ADDIE, Borg and Gall, Procedural, and 4D . A surge in research activity was identified in  2021 , with geometry—specifically three-dimensional shapes—emerging as the dominant research focus. Furthermore, the findings demonstrate that the integration of technology, culture, and subject matter characteristics plays a crucial role in enhancing the meaningfulness of learning for students, teachers, and media developers. This study highlights the need for subsequent research to focus on aligning educational content and technology with specific learning needs and the availability of technological devices.

keywords: Android, Ethnomathematics, Mathematics, Learning Media, Systematic Literature Review
